world.name: STATIC City
world.principle: Unreal Engine 5.8 game-first world layer with social media interfaces, not a website with game controls.
world.currentSlice: Browser owner prototype route: STATIC Tower command suite -> valet -> luxury SUV -> Market Walk -> Radio Rooftop VIP -> vibe-mode strip cruise -> tower return. Full canon moves Mr. Stone primary residence to Private STATIC Island.

tiers.name: STATIC Tower Summit
tiers.copy: The symbolic top: city command landmark, concert crown, theme park, observation deck, and celebrity red-carpet destination. Mr. Stone’s true residence moves to Private STATIC Island.

tiers.name: Crown Ridge
tiers.copy: Luxury hotels, villas, elite lounges, galleries, high-status event routes, and prestige property approaches.

tiers.name: Neon Rise
tiers.copy: Better apartments, Channels, studios, Radio approaches, PLAY lounges, nicer cars, and visible social status.

tiers.name: Base Loop
tiers.copy: The circular strip around the mountain: valet routes, Market Walk, starter businesses, transit, and public venue access.

tiers.name: Street Pulse
tiers.copy: Darker, louder, cheaper, crowded, accessible, and alive with creator hustle, open Signals, street music, and first homes.

routeNodes.label: Tower Command Suite
routeNodes.objective: Prototype wake route in the STATIC Tower command suite. Full canon starts from Mr. Stone’s Private STATIC Island estate once the UE5.8 world exists.

routeNodes.label: Penthouse Elevator
routeNodes.objective: Walk out of the penthouse and enter the private elevator down to the tower valet floor.

routeNodes.label: STATIC Tower
routeNodes.objective: Anchor the city with the flagship venue, event crown, theme park, and command view.

routeNodes.label: Tower Valet
routeNodes.objective: Exit the elevator, wait for the luxury SUV, enter, and start STATIC Radio.

routeNodes.label: Crown Ridge
routeNodes.objective: Pass villas, hotels, elite lounges, red carpets, and high-status social architecture.

routeNodes.label: Mountain Descent
routeNodes.objective: Cruise through brighter mid-city roads while Radio and billboards react to live events.

routeNodes.label: Radio Building
routeNodes.objective: Pull up to the STATIC Radio building, step out, walk in, and take the elevator to the rooftop party.

routeNodes.label: PLAY Gate
routeNodes.objective: Enter playable worlds, limited modes, creator arenas, and future generated game sessions.

routeNodes.label: STATIC Arena
routeNodes.objective: Basketball becomes the first sports proof: arena nights, fan heat, VIP boxes, creator teams, and later playable 1v1/3v3/5v5.

routeNodes.label: Club STATIC
routeNodes.objective: Party, aftershows, sports watch parties, creator appearances, VIP tables, and social gravity on the strip.

routeNodes.label: Market Walk
routeNodes.objective: Stop at the mall, buy new clothes and jewelry, update outfit/status, then continue to STATIC Radio.

routeNodes.label: Radio Rooftop VIP
routeNodes.objective: Get escorted to VIP, receive a drink and cigar, and sit on the couch while the rooftop party moves around him.

routeNodes.label: Vibe Mode Strip
routeNodes.objective: Roll the window down, switch to first-person camera, and cruise past STATIC Strip skyscrapers while STATIC Blues plays.

routeNodes.label: Signal Strip
routeNodes.objective: Open Signals, follow Entities, discover drops, watch clips, and return to the city loop.

routeNodes.label: The Red Light
routeNodes.objective: Find the tucked-away bar, enter through the front, cross the interior, and exit through the back into the secret mature-nightlife walking street.

routeNodes.label: Hidden Walking Street
routeNodes.objective: Prototype the dense neon walking-street energy: bars, lounges, performer balconies, food stalls, coded doors, VIP stairways, crowd heat, and rain-reflection spectacle.
routeNodes.backend: age_gate_later, venue_access, npc_crowds, events, moderation, discovery_flags

routeNodes.label: Velocity Garage
routeNodes.objective: Build the garage identity loop: paint, rims, underglow, suspension, interior, audio, wraps, and saved vehicle builds.

routeNodes.label: Night Run Light
routeNodes.objective: Prototype red-light challenges where an NPC or future real player pulls up beside you and launches an instanced race route.

routeNodes.label: Car Show Plaza
routeNodes.objective: Host car shows promoted through Signals with custom cars, lowriders, slabs, lifted trucks, bikes, motorcycles, stage performances, clips, and drops.

routeNodes.label: Takeover Zone
routeNodes.objective: Controlled fictional underground zone for donuts, burnout choreography, passenger-camera shots, crowd heat, and replay clips.

routeNodes.label: Companion Hub
routeNodes.objective: Choose cute sidekicks, guardian bodyguards, robots, creatures, dragons, golems, companion stance, follow distance, and loadout slots.

routeNodes.label: Guardian Yard
routeNodes.objective: Prototype guardian follow/protect behavior for opt-in danger modes, crew zones, faction arenas, and high-status owner entrances.

routeNodes.label: Crew HQ
routeNodes.objective: Prototype crew formation, crew identity, roles, banners, home base, and moderated faction presence.

routeNodes.label: Dominion Arena
routeNodes.objective: Prototype instanced crew-versus-crew spectacle with fictional loadouts, territory prestige, non-cash Signal Credits, and no public real-value wagering.

routeNodes.label: Fortress Row
routeNodes.objective: Prototype district upgrades: walls, gates, shields, turrets, watch decks, command rooms, and treaty-bloc visual control.

routeNodes.label: Survival Zone
routeNodes.objective: Prototype wave-defense events where crews defend against fictional NPC waves in first-person or third-person arcade mode.

routeNodes.label: Tower Return
routeNodes.objective: Third-person far shot of the SUV going back up the mountain, then Mr. Stone gets out, tips valet, and walks back in.

penthouse.label: Penthouse interior
penthouse.copy: The first playable scene starts inside Mr. Stone’s top-floor penthouse: glass skyline wall, wardrobe/status area, private hallway, and elevator target.

valet.label: Tower valet floor
valet.copy: The first exterior-interior bridge: elevator opens to valet, SUV arrives, radio state starts, and NPC valet logic will live here later.

vehicle.label: Vehicle route
vehicle.copy: Vehicle movement is still authored. Real driving comes after avatar, camera, route, and scene streaming foundations are stable.

market.label: Market Walk retail
market.copy: Market Walk proves the fit, jewelry, inventory, and status loop before the Radio Rooftop arrival.

venue.label: Venue approach
venue.copy: Venue spaces are where NPCs, crowds, props, music, access, and social presence start becoming gameplay.

city.label: STATIC City exterior
city.copy: The broad city layer remains a world map shell until we have authored street chunks, avatar traversal, vehicles, and optimization.

systems: STATIC ID
One account connects web, mobile, future Unreal client, console, VR, haptics, Entity ownership, inventory, companions, vehicles, and event access.
Create-a-player
Face/body builder, wardrobe, jewelry, animations, mic identity, optional voice effects, and persistence.
Property/spawn
Starter rooms, apartments, businesses, penthouses, mansions, castles, suites, and landmark spawn rules.
Vehicle/travel
Valet, vehicle classes, route selection, cinematic travel clips, and later controllable driving.
STATIC Sports
Basketball first: arena nights, fan heat, VIP watch parties, creator teams, and playable sports only after real physics/netcode exist.
Velocity/racing
Red-light challenges, deep car customization, night runs, car shows, takeover zones, and non-cash reputation rewards before any real-value wagering.
Dominion/factions
Crews, alliances, district-control windows, fortress upgrades, treaty blocs, arcade loadouts, and survival-wave events as opt-in game modes.
Companions/guardians
Cute sidekicks, guardian bodyguards, robots, dragons, golems, companion squads, stance, loadouts, and opt-in protection behavior.
Story campaign
GTA-style cinematic open-world chapters with better controls, more realistic graphics, and STATIC creator/social systems; origin missions prove the first slice.
Enterable interiors
Hero interiors are authored, repeatable interiors are templated, towers use lobby/elevator/instanced floors, and background buildings stay facade-only until needed.
Mall/marketplace
Clothing, jewelry, skins, accessories, memberships, wishlists, drops, and payments later.
Radio rooftop
Ambient venue, station UI, now playing, lounge props, and rights-safe/static-made music.
Social presence
NPC shells, friends/follows, then real multiplayer only after auth, privacy, and moderation.
Venue events
Concerts, red carpets, premieres, creator drops, crowd heat, and Signals aftershock.
Inventory/status
Visible fit, vehicle, property, event attendance, access passes, and progression state.
backendContracts: profiles
Supabase user profile, display name, handle, role, privacy, moderation status.
platform_links
Cross-platform login links for web, mobile, PC/console, VR, haptic devices, and future game clients.
entities
Account-bound Entity identity, body/style choices, current spawn, active outfit, mic/effects settings.
entity_loadouts
Active outfit, vehicle, property, companion slots, status tier, and visibility state for a playable Entity.
property_access
Approved/owned spawn locations, businesses, hotel suites, penthouses, mansions, and landmark rights.
inventory_items
Wardrobe, jewelry, props, vehicles, passes, skins, and future paid entitlements.
world_sessions
Current route, scene, position, camera state, client type, vehicle, property, and session status across web/game clients.
signals_social
Follows, saves, comments, public posts, clips, drop announcements, and event aftershock.
radio_sessions
Station schedule, now playing, host, rights source, simulcast, and user listening state.
presence_events
NPC shells, friend presence, room occupancy, event heat, reports, bans, and moderation gates.
story_missions
Mission definitions, chapter arc, beat order, cinematic intent, unlock rules, district dependencies, reward intent, and public/internal visibility.
mission_progress
Per-account mission state, active beat, completed beats, failed/abandoned state, replay permission, and reward issuance.
building_interiors
Interior type, building link, hero/template/lobby/facade/event tier, access rules, streaming target, and moderation status.
interior_instances
Instanced apartment/shop/office/hotel-room state, furniture template, owner access, spawn markers, and reset rules.
age_gates
Mature venue access policy, consent/version state, moderation locks, region/legal flags, and audit trail for hidden nightlife content.
vehicle_builds
Account-bound vehicle ownership/customization, paint, wraps, wheels, underglow, suspension, audio, interior, and moderation state.
race_sessions
Instanced race route, challenger type, non-cash stake, route validation, result, replay pointer, and anti-cheat status later.
sports_events
Arena schedules, sport type, teams, venue route, spectator state, rights/moderation notes, and event heat.
arena_sessions
Playable or watch-party sports session state, participants, score shell, court route, replay pointer, and validation status later.
team_rosters
Creator/studio/crew team membership, role, jersey/skin reference, moderation state, and league-season eligibility.
crews
Crew profile, owner, roles, banner, home base, moderation status, privacy, recruitment state, and seasonal reputation.
territory_control
Seasonal district-control state, treaty bloc, visual ownership, contested windows, cooldowns, and reset schedule.
conflict_sessions
Opt-in arcade conflict instance, crew participants, ruleset, loadout class, result validation, replay pointer, and anti-cheat status later.
fortress_upgrades
Non-cash district visual upgrades, walls, gates, shields, turrets, command rooms, event stage, and entitlement limits.
companions
Account-bound companion identity, species/type, scale class, current skin, animation set, owner, moderation state, and safety flags.
companion_slots
Active companion/bodyguard slots, follow/protect mode, district permissions, performance budget, and opt-in conflict availability.

visualTargets.title: Arrival District fidelity
visualTargets.copy: The world should feel like the generated Arrival District: dense, cinematic, social, expensive, wet streets, moving light, and venue signage everywhere.

visualTargets.title: Signals as street culture
visualTargets.copy: Signals should become people, phones, billboards, drops, clips, crowd heat, and creator pulse inside the city.

visualTargets.title: Radio as destination
visualTargets.copy: Radio is not a playlist screen. It is a rooftop venue, broadcast desk, skyline, crowd, host layer, and Vibe Mode soundtrack.

visualTargets.title: PLAY as a portal district
visualTargets.copy: STATIC PLAY should feel like walking into playable worlds, arenas, lounges, limited modes, and creator-made game entrances.

visualTargets.title: Arena and fan gravity
visualTargets.copy: The Arrival District image already implies sports nights, VIP tables, fan movement, and arena heat. Basketball should be the first playable sport lane.
